text.title: iac-patterns-testing Core Rules
text.summary: Migrated testable rules for iac-patterns-testing. Verify and refine before relying on for production.

rule.statement: Level 1 — Static Analysis: run terraform fmt, terraform validate, and tflint on every PR. Also run checkov or tfsec for security scanning. These must pass before any plan.
rule.rationale: Static analysis catches structural errors (missing required arguments, deprecated syntax, misconfigured provider blocks) in seconds without cloud credentials. They are the lowest-cost gate.

rule.statement: Level 2 — Unit Testing: use native terraform test (.tftest.hcl files in tests/) with command = plan and mock providers. Test: default inputs produce correct plan, all input validations reject invalid values, outputs match expected values.
rule.rationale: Plan-only tests with mocks run in seconds, require no cloud credentials, and validate the module's contract without creating real resources.

rule.statement: Level 3 — Integration Testing: use Terratest (Go) to deploy real resources in a dedicated test environment. Always defer terraform.Destroy to ensure cleanup. Validate resource properties via cloud SDK calls after apply.
rule.rationale: Only integration tests catch issues that surface only after apply: IAM propagation delays, service quotas, cross-resource dependency ordering, and eventual-consistency bugs.

rule.statement: Level 4 — Policy Testing: use OPA/Conftest or Sentinel to enforce compliance rules (encryption required, naming conventions, required tags, no public access). Policy tests run against the plan output (JSON) before apply.
rule.rationale: Policy-as-code makes compliance requirements testable and auditable; a Conftest rule that blocks public S3 buckets is more reliable than a wiki page or manual review.
